questions.prompt: Which tense combination is correct for a Type 2 (hypothetical) conditional?
options: si + past perfect subjunctive + conditional perfect
questions.explanation: Type 2 hypothetical: si + past subjunctive + conditional. For example: 'Si tuviera dinero, viajaría.'

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Si ___ (saber, nosotros) cocinar, ___ (hacer) la cena en casa.'
questions.explanation: Type 2 hypothetical: si + past subjunctive (supiéramos) + conditional (haríamos). Implies we don't know how to cook.

questions.prompt: What type of conditional is: 'Si hubiera llegado antes, habría visto el partido.'?
questions.explanation: Type 3: si + past perfect subjunctive (hubiera llegado) + conditional perfect (habría visto). The event didn't happen — the person didn't arrive in time.

questions.prompt: Which is the correct Mexican Spanish phrase for an informal suggestion?
options: Si vamos al cine esta noche.
¿Y si vamos al cine esta noche?
Cuando vayamos al cine esta noche.
questions.explanation: '¿Y si + present indicative?' is the common Mexican Spanish formula for casual suggestions, equivalent to 'What if we...' or 'How about we...'

questions.prompt: Why is 'Si tengas tiempo, llámame' INCORRECT?
options: You should use the future tense: tendrás
The present subjunctive is never used after 'si' in conditional sentences
It should be 'si tuvieras' because time is hypothetical
You cannot use 'llamar' in conditional sentences
questions.explanation: The present subjunctive (tengas) is never used after 'si' in conditional clauses. Use present indicative (tienes) for real Type 1 conditions or past subjunctive (tuvieras) for hypothetical Type 2.

options: Si estudiaras más, sacarías mejores notas.
Si estudias más, sacarás mejores notas.
Si hubieras estudiado más, habrías sacado mejores notas.
Si estudiaras más, habrías sacado mejores notas.
questions.explanation: Type 1: si + present indicative (estudias) + future (sacarás). This is a real, likely conditional — if you study more, you will get better grades.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses a mixed conditional (past condition → present result)?
options: Si hubiera estudiado medicina, sería médica ahora.
questions.explanation: Mixed: si + past perfect subjunctive (hubiera estudiado — past condition) + conditional (sería — present result). She didn't study medicine in the past, so she isn't a doctor now.

questions.prompt: What does 'por si acaso' mean in Mexican Spanish?
questions.explanation: 'Por si acaso' is a very common Mexican Spanish expression meaning 'just in case'. It is used when taking a precaution without being sure the situation will arise.
